summary: Made Path Map route nodes clickable and added a Node Intel focus panel so checkpoints, gates, and unlocks can be inspected without leaving the route board.

queries: timeline drilldown details panel UX patterns dashboard
interactive timeline focus panel milestones blockers dashboard UX
roadmap dashboard click milestone detail panel design pattern
pattern_notes: Dashboard drilldowns should reveal detail near the overview instead of replacing the user's context.
Timeline and roadmap components commonly use hover/click milestone states plus adjacent details.
Operational dashboards need quick scan hierarchy first, then selected-node detail for decisions and next actions.

ui_contract: Give every Path Map node a stable local id derived from source data or fallback index.
Render route nodes as focusable buttons with aria-pressed for the currently inspected node.
Default the Node Intel panel to the first active gate, then incomplete node, then spawn.
Show selected node title, kind/status/level tags, route progress, detail body, local note preview when available, and contextual action buttons.
Keep focus state browser-memory only and avoid mutating the snapshot.

red_route.evidence: Before implementation there was no .path-node-intel panel and no data-path-node-focus controls.

green_route.evidence: The Path Map rendered a Node Intel panel, 13 focus controls, exactly one focused/pressed route node, click selection changed the panel to Startup Memo, NEXT advanced it to Local Proof, and there were no console errors, failed requests, or overflow.

responsive.evidence: All tested viewports rendered the Node Intel panel, changed focus after a route-node click, kept exactly one pressed node, and reported no bad rectangles or horizontal overflow.
